nutrient_source.title: S.Pellegrino Sparkling Natural Mineral Water — USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods contains an entry for S.Pellegrino sparkling natural mineral water confirming 0 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 0 g carbohydrate, 0 g sugar, 0 g dietary fibre per 100 g, with sodium reported at approximately 10.6 mg per 100 ml per product label data. Mineral content (calcium, magnesium) sourced from S.Pellegrino official product specification and cross-referenced with USDA entry.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Water and Non-Caloric Beverages — University of Sydney GI Database Convention
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure mineral water contains no glycemic carbohydrates and does not raise blood glucose. The University of Sydney GI Database does not assign a formal GI to water as it contains no testable carbohydrate load. Per established convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category is set to low. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g is 0.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (10.6 mg per 100 ml)
hypertension.guidance: San Pellegrino sparkling mineral water contains approximately 10.6 mg sodium per 100 ml, which is well within safe limits for hypertensive individuals; adequate hydration with low-sodium mineral water is generally beneficial for blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.guidance: San Pellegrino sparkling mineral water contains no carbohydrates or sugars and has a glycemic load of zero, making it a safe and recommended hydration choice for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero cholesterol, low sodium, trace minerals
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: San Pellegrino sparkling mineral water contains no fat, no cholesterol, and negligible sodium, and provides trace amounts of calcium and magnesium which may support cardiovascular health; it is a heart-healthy hydration option.

llm_training.natural_language_description: San Pellegrino (S.Pellegrino) is a naturally sparkling mineral water sourced from a spring in San Pellegrino Terme, in the Lombardy region of northern Italy. The water is naturally carbonated at its source and is bottled by Nestlé Waters under the S.Pellegrino brand. It is one of the most widely recognised premium sparkling mineral waters in the world and is commonly served in upscale restaurants, hotels, and cafes globally, including in Singapore where it is available through food delivery platforms and dining establishments. The water has a crisp, clean taste with fine, persistent bubbles and a slightly mineral character derived from its natural mineral content, including calcium (approximately 173 mg/L), magnesium (approximately 51 mg/L), and sodium (approximately 106 mg/L). It contains zero calories, zero protein, zero fat, and zero carbohydrates, making it nutritionally inert from a macronutrient perspective. It is suitable for all dietary patterns including vegan, vegetarian, halal, kosher, and gluten-free diets. Clinically, it is safe for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease due to its absence of sugar, fat, and cholesterol, and its low sodium content. The 500 ml bottle format is the standard single-serve size commonly encountered in restaurant settings in Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in San Pellegrino sparkling water?
Is San Pellegrino water good for diabetics?
What minerals are in San Pellegrino water?
Can I drink San Pellegrino water if I have high blood pressure?
What is the sodium content of San Pellegrino?
Is sparkling mineral water the same as soda water?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods entry for S.Pellegrino sparkling natural mineral water, which is an approved secondary global source. Mineral content cross-referenced with official S.Pellegrino product specification. All core macros are confirmed zero by the approved source. GI convention for zero-carbohydrate beverages is well-established.
notes: energy_kcal is naturally zero as sparkling mineral water contains no caloric macronutrients. protein_g is naturally zero as mineral water contains no protein by nature. fat_g is naturally zero as mineral water contains no fat by nature. carbohydrate_g is naturally zero as mineral water contains no carbohydrates by nature. sugar_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as water contains no dietary fibre. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are confirmed zero as the product contains no fat. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for mineral water. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this product. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. The cuisine tag in the input JSON listed Middle Eastern due to the serving restaurant (Sahara Bar and Restaurant); however, San Pellegrino is an Italian product and cuisine has been corrected to Italian / International. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore.

Documented all -1 sentinel values with explicit reason statements in notes.
